I AM ME

A song about belonging, voice, and becoming who you’re meant to be.

▶ Play Song ⇩ Teacher Card
♫ 3:28
☻ Voice & Belonging
I am me—
I belong.
I have a voice—
I am strong.

I can try,
I can grow—
I am learning
who I am.
✿
♡
I Am Me Identity, dignity, belonging, voice, and becoming.
Upload file as audio/i-am-me.mp3 to activate this player.
♡ About This Song

“I Am Me” celebrates every child’s unique voice. It helps children feel safe to express themselves, value their differences, and belong—just as they are.

☆

Builds Identity

Affirms each child’s unique strengths and qualities.

♧

Encourages Voice

Supports children in finding their voice—in their own way.

♡

Strengthens Belonging

Creates a classroom culture where everyone can be seen and heard.

♧

Student View

Project these lyrics for your class.
I am me— I belong. I have a voice— I am strong.
I can try, I can grow— I am learning who I am.

Some voices come fast, some voices take time.
Some voices are quiet— and still they shine.

Side by side, we all show— we are learning who we are.

Just like this… I begin.
⛶ Fullscreen ▣ Print Lyrics
🍎

Teacher Tools

Ways to use this song in your classroom.
♧

Community Circle

Use the song to open or close circle time.

☏

Voice Sharing

Invite students to share how they speak, express, or listen.

♡

Affirming Differences

Celebrate what makes each of us unique.

✎

Identity Reflection

Draw, write, or collage about “one thing that makes me me.”

♧

Partner Talk

“Some voices are quiet—and still they shine.” What does that mean?

♫

Movement & Expression

Move or gesture to show how you feel.

♡ Next Steps
♫

1. Listen Together

Enjoy the song and notice which lines resonate most.

♡

2. Connect

Invite students to share or reflect in ways that feel safe.

♧

3. Create

Express identity through art, words, movement, or sound.

♧

4. Celebrate

Honor every voice. We all belong here.

Teacher Note ♡

You are helping children learn that they don’t have to be anyone else to belong.

That is powerful.
♡

Full Lyrics

Use for singing, projection, or reflection.
Chorus

I am me—
I belong.
I have a voice—
I am strong.

I can try,
I can grow—
I am learning
who I am.

Verse 1

Some days I feel quiet,
some days I feel loud,
some days I feel unsure,
some days I feel proud.

All of me is welcome,
every part I bring—
I don’t have to hide it,
I can be seen.

Chorus

I am me—
I belong.
I have a voice—
I am strong.

I can try,
I can grow—
I am learning
who I am.

Verse 2

My voice doesn’t have to
sound like someone else,
I can speak in my way,
I can be myself.

Every word I’m finding,
every step I take—
is part of my story,
part of what I make.

Bridge

Some voices come fast,
some voices take time.
Some voices are quiet—
and still they shine.

Final Chorus

I am me—
I belong.
I have a voice—
I am strong.

Side by side,
we all show—
we are learning
who we are.

Outro

Just like this…
I begin.

Listen & Sing

I Am Me · Classroom Version

▶
3:28

Teacher Cue

“You don’t have to sound like anyone else to belong here. Your voice can be quiet, loud, unsure, proud, or still becoming.”

Best Moments to Use

Morning circle, identity work, buddy class meetings, before sharing, after conflict repair, or anytime students need affirmation.

Student Frame

“One thing that makes me me is _______.”
“My voice shines when _______.”

☆

First Week Pathway

A gentle rollout for voice, identity, and belonging.
1

Listen & Notice

Play the chorus. Ask students to notice one word or line that feels important.

2

Name Belonging

Use the frame: “I belong when _______.” Students may say, draw, or gesture.

3

Explore Voice

Discuss quiet, loud, unsure, and proud voices. All can belong.

4

Create Identity

Students make a small identity card, symbol, drawing, or “I am me” line.

5

Celebrate Together

Sing the final chorus and honor one way the class is learning who they are.

✎

Reflection Prompts

Use orally, visually, or in journals.
I am learning that I am _______.
My voice sounds like _______.
One part of me I want people to see is _______.
Some voices are quiet—and still they shine means _______.
I can help someone belong by _______.
✿

Student Expression Extensions

Small projects that let identity become visible.

Identity Collage

Students combine images, words, colors, and symbols that represent who they are.

Voice Map

Students draw where their voice feels quiet, brave, playful, strong, or still growing.

Belonging Chain

Each student adds one sentence about belonging to a classroom paper chain.

New Verse

Students write or co-create an extra verse using the pattern “Some days I…”
